observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: no_success_like_trajectories; partial_progress count dropped from 50 to 17 in long training
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: memory coverage still shows 124 partial_progress trajectories, but candidate training regressed

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: parent reward consistently generates partial_progress (124 in memory, 50 in previous training) but no success_like trajectories; plateau in partial_progress conversion
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: local_repair mutation to strengthen terminal condition rewards (safe_landing_bonus) and reduce ineffective components (stability_penalty, crash_penalty, etc.) while preserving approach_progress and near_stage_stability
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: long training produced only 17 partial_progress, 20 ambiguous, and 9 early_failure; training return dropped from 166 to 20.9; no success_like trajectories emerged; the repair disrupted partial_progress generation without achieving progress toward success

lesson: Local repair that simultaneously removes multiple components and modifies terminal conditions can destabilize the existing partial_progress behavior; preserving the ability to generate partial_progress is a prerequisite before attempting to bridge to success; incremental, single-component changes with validation of partial_progress retention are safer.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress component as it is the primary driver of partial_progress
avoid simultaneous removal or gating of multiple components; test each change individually
before introducing new success mechanisms, ensure the reward function maintains at least the same number of partial_progress trajectories as the parent
investigate ambiguous trajectories: they may indicate reward misalignment and should be minimized
do not treat ambiguous trajectories as progress in preference construction
consider using a conservative mutation that adds a mild success bonus without altering existing components
re-evaluate the necessity of removing components that were previously helpful (e.g., crash_penalty, stability_penalty) as their removal may degrade partial_progress

archive_update.reason: Candidate was selected, trained, and added to elite archive despite regression; next round should consider reverting to a parent that preserved partial_progress (e.g., round2 or round3 elite) and apply more conservative mutations.

uncertainties: The regression in partial_progress may be due to stochasticity in training or the mutation itself; the candidate's TAGE score on memory was positive (0.63), so the memory evaluation did not predict the training outcome
No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed
The high number of ambiguous trajectories (20) indicates the reward function may be poorly calibrated for the policy's actual behavior
Decision level was weak_pairwise_selection, so all conclusions are tentative and may not generalize
